q: Which team uses facial recognition in their stadium?
True or false: facial recognition is used by every sports team.
What can facial recognition do to help a team?
opts: Get players more things that they want

q: How is facial recognition technology most heavily utilized regarding stadium security and staff?
opts: Monitoring player heart rates during high-stress moments
Authenticating credentialed staff entering restricted areas like locker rooms
Scoring the performance of referees and officials

q: Under what real-world stadium conditions does facial recognition experience the highest drop in accuracy?
opts: Empty stadiums with bright overhead lighting
Moving crowds with shadows and varying viewing angles
Indoor press conferences with fixed cameras

q: What is a primary concern raised by privacy watchdogs regarding biometric data collection at sports venues?
opts: It is completely mandatory for all ticket holders
It is strictly opt-in for fans who choose to use it
It is only available to VIPs and suite owners
It requires a physical fingerprint scan alongside the facial scan

q: Which of the following is NOT a use of facial recognition?
opts: Monitoring changes in a person during medical treatment
A computer measures the distance of facial features

q: We do not have a legal right to privacy in the facial features we show in public.
